vitals.location: Clare and the Clare Valley wine region, South Australia, Australia
vitals.date: No edition held since 2018; the planned 2020 return was cancelled for COVID-19, and no future edition has been announced

climate.description: South Australian autumn heat is a real factor — the 2018 edition saw 33-36°C with hot, gusty northerlies.
challenges: Gusty northerly crosswinds — organizer materials warned deep-section wheels could be affected

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 54. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 53. EDITORIAL WAVE 10 FLAG (2026-07-24): this profile's on-file date (Feb 15 2026) actually belongs to a different race entirely (Spain's Clásica de Almería) and the youtube_data/rider_intel described Ireland's ŠKODA Ring of Clare Cycle, not this event. No edition has run since 2018 (the only edition ever held); the planned 2020 return was cancelled for COVID-19 and never rescheduled. field_depth/prestige/organization dimensions below were inherited from the contaminated profile and have not been re-evaluated — flagging for human rescoring given the event has held only one edition. No numeric changes made this wave.

course_description.character: The last confirmed edition (2018) ran as an open, undulating road gran fondo through the Clare Valley wine region roughly 140km north of Adelaide — vineyards, sweeping valley views, and historic rural towns rather than one defining climb. Terrain was described as rolling and punchy: frequent short rises and pinches over ~1,600m across the 160km Maxi course, not an alpine profile. Roads were semi-closed, not fully closed — the organizer's own materials later identified a different event (2022 Snowy Classic) as its first fully closed-road race.
course_description.signature_challenge: No named KOM or signature climb is documented — the character is sustained rolling wine-country terrain and heat/wind exposure rather than a single defining ascent.
suffering_zones: Gusty northerly crosswinds across open valley roads — organizer materials warned deep-section wheels could be affected
Mintaro and the surrounding rolling sections — one contemporary rider report described the 110km course as harder than the organizer's Bowral Classic event

history.origin_story: Announced September 2017 as the second gran fondo in The Classics series (Yaffa Media/Bicycling Australia, founded 2016; Bowral Classic in New South Wales is the series' flagship). Debuted Sunday, 8 April 2018 with roughly 900 riders across 160/110/50km courses, about 40% from interstate. A second edition — including a planned Saturday 'Dirty Clare' 80km gravel fondo — was scheduled for April 2020 but cancelled 16 March 2020 due to COVID-19. No edition has run since, and the organizer's current site shows only stale/misapplied date copy for Clare while Bowral and Mudgee carry live 2026 dates.
history.reputation: The organizer's own post-event coverage described the 2018 debut as a major success; no edition has run since.

biased_opinion.verdict: No Edition Since 2018 — Don't Plan a Trip Around This
biased_opinion.summary: Clare Classic held one edition — 8 April 2018, roughly 900 riders through Clare Valley wine country, ~40% from interstate. The planned 2020 return was cancelled for COVID-19 and never rescheduled; no edition has run since, and the organizer's current site shows only stale, misapplied date text for Clare while sibling events Bowral and Mudgee carry live 2026 dates. This profile previously carried Ireland's ŠKODA Ring of Clare Cycle footage and an incorrect Spanish race date — corrected this wave (see catalog_flags). What's documented is a single well-organized debut with no announced path back to the calendar.
strengths: The one confirmed edition (2018): ~900 riders, ~40% interstate, rolling Clare Valley wine-country roads
Three distance options (160/110/50km) made it accessible to a range of riders
Part of an established organizer series (The Classics/Bicycling Australia, also behind Bowral)
weaknesses: No edition held since 2018 — the planned 2020 return was cancelled for COVID-19 and never rescheduled
Current organizer website shows stale, misapplied date information rather than a clear 'discontinued' or 'returning' statement
Roads were only semi-closed, not fully closed, in the one edition that ran
Significant heat exposure risk (33-36°C recorded in 2018) with gusty crosswinds
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A single well-organized 2018 debut with no edition since — don't book travel around it without confirming directly with the organizer first.

final_verdict.one_liner: A 2018 Clare Valley gran fondo debut with no edition held since — the planned 2020 return was cancelled and never rescheduled.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Not currently — there's no confirmed 2026 edition. Contact the organizer (The Classics) directly before planning any trip around this event.
final_verdict.alternatives: For an active event from the same organizer: Bowral Classic in New South Wales.

citations.label: Bicycling Australia — Planned 2020 'Dirty Clare' Gravel Add-On (never ran)

eligibility.notes: Only one edition ever ran (8 April 2018, ~900 riders). The planned 2020 edition (with a Saturday 'Dirty Clare' gravel add-on) was cancelled 16 March 2020 for COVID-19 and has not been rescheduled or relaunched as of 2026 (no edition held since 2018) — organizer's current Clare page shows only stale/misapplied date text ('Sunday, 19 October 2025') while sibling events Bowral and Mudgee carry live 2026 dates. No explicit discontinuation notice found, so status is unknown rather than defunct. Prior on-file date (Feb 15 2026) actually belonged to Spain's Clásica de Almería (UCI record), a wholly different, unrelated race. via codex gpt-5.6-sol web research, editorial wave 10.

catalog_flags.status_note: Identity-corrected this wave (2026-07-24): all prior youtube_data/quotes/rider_intel (ŠKODA Ring of Clare Cycle, ŠKODA Boyne Valley Classic — both Irish events near Ennis, County Clare and Drogheda) were unrelated contamination; Ring of Clare was name-confusable ('Clare' in County Clare, Ireland vs. Clare Valley, South Australia), while Boyne Valley Classic was simply unrelated. The on-file date (Feb 15 2026) also belonged to a different, unrelated race (Spain's Clásica de Almería). This is the real, separate South Australian Clare Classic (Clare Valley, ~140km north of Adelaide, Bicycling Australia's Classics series), whose only confirmed edition ran in 2018; the planned 2020 edition was cancelled for COVID-19 and no edition has run since. All youtube_data removed as non-matching.
